evidence: We are devoting significant capital and management time and resources to using AI technologies to improve our products, services and controls, and to build and expand our capabilities.
We have devoted and plan to continue devoting substantial time, money and management resources to developing new technologies, products and services.
We continue to invest in AI-enhanced listing capabilities, advanced advertising solutions, global shipping improvements and flexible payment options.
We continue to expand AI across the listing workflow, using Generative AI to prefill item details, generate optimized titles and descriptions and in some cases recognize product images.

Our AI-enhanced features and services power dynamic product recommendation feeds within our enhanced discovery platform, as well as our AI shopping agent pilot that delivers personalized search results.
AI-enhanced discovery tools and interactive live shopping experiences through eBay Live... one-click card grading and resale for trading cards through our partnership with PSA.
EU AI Act, which lays out the parameters for AI systems where non-compliance can result in fines up to 35 million euros or 7% of global turnover, came into force in August 2024.

AI enhanced products could produce inaccurate, misleading, biased or false outcomes (e.g., hallucinatory behavior currently seen with Gen AI models) or other unexpected results that could harm our reputation.
Hackers have unsuccessfully targeted us using an AI-generated voice impersonation of our CEO. We expect cyberattacks utilizing AI to continue and evolve.

limitations: No quantified dollar amounts or headcount figures tied specifically to AI investment were found in the excerpts; investment language is qualitative ('significant capital', 'substantial time, money'), preventing a score of 4 or 5 for investment intensity.

AI narrative concreteness scored 4 based on named deployed features (Gen AI listing prefill, AI shopping agent pilot, recommendation feeds) with operational detail, but no quantified business results (e.g., % improvement, volume) were disclosed to justify a score of 5.

Risk disclosure scored 4 due to named company-specific risk mechanisms (regulatory fines with specific amounts, hallucination risk, AI-powered cyberattacks against eBay), but no formal quantified risk governance body or mitigation program was described to justify a score of 5.
